commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR needs contributor proof plus maintainer judgment on the keyless Firecrawl provider boundary; ClawSweeper should not open an autonomous repair lane for an external draft without real behavior proof.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete credential-boundary issue because the new free provider can send existing Firecrawl API keys despite advertising no-key access.; Review finding: [P1] Keep the... (sha=8e3a86e7c7ac29fba4288ccc1312098b721462f6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `firecrawl-free` web_search provider, allows keyless Firecrawl search requests through the client, expands `firecrawl_search` options, and updates plugin metadata, docs, catalog data, doctor migration metadata, and tests.

PR surface: Source +153, Tests +173, Docs +11, Other +15. Total +352 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review findings: source inspection shows `firecrawl-free` passes full config into a client that resolves and sends configured Firecrawl keys, and the same client hard-codes result provider identity to `firecrawl`. A live unauthenticated Firecrawl search from this environment was rejected, so positive end-to-end behavior remains unproven.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor proof plus maintainer judgment on the keyless Firecrawl provider boundary; ClawSweeper should not open an autonomous repair lane for an external draft without real behavior proof.

target.title: feat(firecrawl): keyless web_search + richer firecrawl_search options
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Firecrawl `web_fetch`/`firecrawl_scrape` already support hosted **keyless** starter access (PR #93590), but `web_search` still hard-required a Firecrawl API key. That means OpenClaw could fetch pages out-of-the-box but could not search the web without a key, an inconsistent gap. The hosted `/v2/search` endpoint accepts the same unauthenticated starter requests as `/v2/scrape`.

The explicit `firecrawl_search` tool was also missing several first-class `/v2/search` knobs (domain filters, time filter, geo-targeting) and capped `count` at 10 vs the API's 100.

## Why This Change Was Made

Mirror the existing keyless scrape pattern for search, exactly:
- `runFirecrawlSearch` now allows a missing key only when `access === "keyless"` (same gate as `runFirecrawlScrape`).
- The generic `web_search` provider opts into `access: "keyless"`; the explicit `firecrawl_search` tool stays key-required (same split as `web_fetch` vs `firecrawl_scrape`).
- The search provider declares `requiresCredential: false`, so the existing keyless-aware onboarding (`src/flows/search-setup.ts`) and install catalog adapt with no flow changes.
- Core `resolveRuntimeWebTools` sets `allowKeylessAutoSelect: true` for search (kept `deferKeylessFallback: true`, so any credentialed provider still wins; keyless Firecrawl is the no-config fallback).

Additive `firecrawl_search` options were brought to parity with `/v2/search`: `includeDomains`/`excludeDomains` (mutually exclusive), `tbs`, `location`, `country`, and `count` raised to 100.

## User Impact

- With no Firecrawl API key, `web_search` now works via hosted keyless starter access (add a key for higher limits). Onboarding shows Firecrawl Search as key-free.
- Users with a configured/keyed search provider are unaffected — keyed providers still take precedence.
- `firecrawl_search` gains domain/time/location filters and up to 100 results.
- No config migration required; key remains optional.

## Evidence

- Live: `POST https://api.firecrawl.dev/v2/search` with no `Authorization` header returns `200` + results, confirming the keyless tier.
- Tests: `firecrawl-tools.test.ts` (keyless search omits auth header; direct search requires key; new filters forward; include/exclude mutual exclusion) and `runtime-web-tools.test.ts` (auto-selects keyless search provider with no creds) pass.
- `tsgo` core + extensions + extension-tests clean; oxlint + oxfmt + docs-format clean.
- Docs updated: `docs/tools/firecrawl.md`, manifest `uiHints`.

Draft while end-to-end onboarding proof on a local build is captured.
target.author: developersdigest
